meta.title: The Roman Catholic Dogma of the Immaculate Conception
meta.author: James White

questions.question: According to White, Luke 1:46–47 (Mary rejoicing in "God my Savior") does not support which idea when read with standard Protestant grammatical-historical exegesis?
options: That Mary needed a Savior like all sinners
A preemptive application of Christ's merits protecting Mary from original sin before her life of faith
That Mary responded to God in faith
questions.answer: A preemptive application of Christ's merits protecting Mary from original sin before her life of faith

questions.question: White contrasts sola scriptura with what descriptive label for ultimate authority resting in the church rather than Scripture alone?
Which passage does White cite as the other New Testament use of the cognate related to κεχαριτωμένη, to argue the word concerns favor/grace rather than sinlessness?
White argues that if perfect passive participles of blessing/grace always implied sinlessness from conception, which kind of reductio would follow for other biblical texts?
options: Angels would have to be conceived immaculately
Believers described with similar forms could be argued "immaculately conceived" as well
The Old Testament saints would be co-redeemers
questions.answer: Believers described with similar forms could be argued "immaculately conceived" as well

questions.question: Which Gospel text does White mention alongside 1 Thessalonians 1:4 and 2 Thessalonians 2:13 as a parallel to participial "blessed/loved" language?
White claims the angel repeats the substance in Luke 1:30 without doing what?
options: Reusing the same "grace" wording as in the greeting
questions.answer: Reusing the same "grace" wording as in the greeting

questions.question: In the lecture's discussion of Genesis 3:15 and Latin, what contrast does White highlight between older Vulgate wording and the Nova Vulgata?
According to White's reading of Ludwig Ott, papal infallibility attaches to the dogmatic definition but not necessarily to what?
options: The reasons or scriptural explanations offered as leading up to the dogma
questions.answer: The reasons or scriptural explanations offered as leading up to the dogma

questions.question: White quotes Ott to the effect that the necessary interpretation of the protoevangelium used in the dogmatic argument is not found where?
options: In the writings of the Reformers
In the writings of the majority of the Fathers, including major Eastern and Western teachers
questions.answer: In the writings of the majority of the Fathers, including major Eastern and Western teachers

questions.question: White argues Luke 1:42 means "blessed among women" rather than what mistaken maximal sense?
Which second-century figure does White quote to the effect that we must not infer God did everything he could do—only what God actually revealed he did?
White says St. Bernard pointed out that suitability arguments, if pressed, would imply what?
options: Festivals for the conceptions of Mary's ancestors without end
questions.answer: Festivals for the conceptions of Mary's ancestors without end

questions.question: White cites J.N.D. Kelly to list which early figures as teaching Mary committed personal sins?
White summarizes Augustine as exempting Mary from personal sin but not from what?
White associates Pelagius with exempting Mary from what Augustine denied for her?
White claims Augustine taught Mary died due to what kind of sin (in his cited line of argument)?
Which 12th-century English monk does White identify with an early form of the Immaculate Conception proposal later rejected by major scholastics?
White says Bernard, Peter Lombard, and Aquinas did what in relation to Eadmer's proposal?
White summarizes Aquinas as arguing that if Mary had no original sin, what would follow about Christ's redemption?
options: Mary would not need Christ as universal redeemer in the same way, undermining Christ as universal redeemer
Christ would not be truly human
Mary would be co-eternal with the Father
questions.answer: Mary would not need Christ as universal redeemer in the same way, undermining Christ as universal redeemer

questions.question: Which pope's 1849 letter (*Ubi primum*) does White discuss as preceding the 1854 definition?
Which archbishop of Paris does White cite as fearing the definition could not be proved from Scripture or tradition?
White cites Philip Schaff as appealing to which patristic "rule" canon (Vincent of Lérins) against the dogma's alleged universality?
White contrasts Dominican and Franciscan conflict over the Immaculate Conception with what expectation if the doctrine were truly apostolic?
options: There would be no need for bishops
The Crusades would not have happened
The Eastern Church would have accepted the Filioque
There would be no medieval debate about it
questions.answer: There would be no medieval debate about it

questions.question: White claims *Ineffabilis Deus* asserts the doctrine had always existed in the church as revealed doctrine; White says this is false because even whom admits it is not explicit in Scripture and not explicitly taught by the Greek and Latin Fathers?
White's closing frame compares binding *de fide* Marian dogma to Paul's warning about adding something small to what?
